```tactics
import Clap.Circuit
import Clap.Simulation
import Clap.Id

namespace Clap

/-
  This file introduces an optimization to remove duplicate checks.
  The current version eliminates any `eq0` with an expression that has
  already been met before. The same optimization should be applicable
  to boolean checks, limb checks and other lookup arguments with minor
  modifications.

  Dedup is extremely valuable as it allows to write composable
  circuits with many redundant checks without having to pay the price
  in term of constraints.
-/

namespace Dedup

variable {p : ÔäĽ} [Fact (Nat.Prime p)]

def to_nat {F var : Type} (e : Exp F (ÔäĽ ├Ś var)) : Exp F ÔäĽ :=
  match e with
  | .v (v, _) => .v v
  | .c f => .c f
  | .add l r => to_nat l + to_nat r
  | .mul l r => to_nat l * to_nat r
  | .sub l r => to_nat l - to_nat r

def to_var {F var : Type} (e : Exp F (ÔäĽ ├Ś var)) : Exp F var :=
  match e with
  | .v (_, v) => .v v
  | .c f => .c f
  | .add l r => to_var l + to_var r
  | .mul l r => to_mul l * to_mul r
  | .sub l r => to_mul l - to_mul r

def beq : (e1 e2 : Exp (ZMod p) Nat) -> Bool
  | .v n1, .v n2
  | .c n1, .c n2 => n1 = n2
  | .add ll lr, .add rl rr => beq ll rl Ôłž beq lr rr
  | .mul ll lr, .mul rl rr => beq ll rl Ôłž beq lr rr
  | .sub ll lr, .sub rl rr => beq ll rl Ôłž beq lr rr
  | _,_ => false

-- TODO we are mixing expressions from eq0 and assert_range, they should be in different sets
def dedup_ {var} (c : Circuit p (ÔäĽ ├Ś var)) (n : ÔäĽ) (set : List (Exp (ZMod p) ÔäĽ)) : Circuit p var :=
  match c with
  | .nil => .nil
  | .eq0 e c =>
    if (to_nat e) Ôłł set
    then dedup_ c n set
    else .eq0 (to_var e) (dedup_ c n ((to_nat e)::set))
  | .lam k => .lam (fun x => dedup_ (k (n,x)) (n+1) set)
  | .share e k => .share (to_var e) (fun x => dedup_ (k (n,x)) (n+1) set)
  | .is_zero e k => .is_zero (to_var e) (fun x => dedup_ (k (n,x)) (n+1) set)
  | .assert_range w e c => .assert_range w (to_var e) (dedup_ c n ((to_nat e)::set))

def dedup {var} (c : Circuit p (Nat ├Ś var)) : Circuit p var := dedup_ c 0 []

def dedup' (c : Circuit' p) : Circuit' p := fun var => dedup (c (Nat ├Ś var))

def expected : Circuit' 7 := fun _ => .lam (fun x => .eq0 (.v x + .c 1) (.eq0 (.v x + .c 2) .nil ))

#guard s!"{dedup' a Nat}" = s!"{expected Nat}"

end Test
```

```tactics
import Clap.Simulation
import Clap.Id

namespace Clap

/-
  This file introduces an optimization to remove duplicate checks.
  The current version eliminates any `eq0` with an expression that has
  already been met before. The same optimization should be applicable
  to boolean checks, limb checks and other lookup arguments with minor
  modifications.

  Dedup is extremely valuable as it allows to write composable
  circuits with many redundant checks without having to pay the price
  in term of constraints.
-/

namespace Dedup

variable {p : ÔäĽ} [Fact (Nat.Prime p)]

def to_nat {F var : Type} (e : Exp F (ÔäĽ ├Ś var)) : Exp F ÔäĽ :=
  match e with
  | .v (v, _) => .v v
  | .c f => .c f
  | .add l r => to_nat l + to_nat r
  | .mul l r => to_nat l * to_nat r
  | .sub l r => to_nat l - to_nat r

def to_var {F var : Type} (e : Exp F (ÔäĽ ├Ś var)) : Exp F var :=
  match e with
  | .v (_, v) => .v v
  | .c f => .c f
  | .add l r => to_var l + to_var r
  | .mul l r => to_mul l r
  | .sub l r => to_sub l r

def beq : (e1 e2 : Exp (ZMod p) Nat) -> Bool
  | .v n1, .v n2
  | .c n1, .c n2 => n1 = n2
  | .add ll lr, .add rl rr => beq ll rl Ôłž beq lr rr
  | .mul ll lr, .mul rl rr => beq ll rl Ôłž beq lr rr
  | .sub ll lr, .sub rl rr => beq ll rl Ôłž beq lr rr
  | _,_ => false

-- TODO we are mixing expressions from eq0 and assert_range, they should be in different sets
def dedup_ {var} (c : Circuit p (ÔäĽ ├Ś var)) (n : ÔäĽ) (set : List (Exp (ZMod p) ÔäĽ)) : Circuit p var :=
  match c with
  | .nil => .nil
  | .eq0 e c =>
    if (to_nat e) Ôłł set
    then dedup_ c n set
    else .eq0 (to_var e) (dedup_ c n ((to_nat e)::set))
  | .lam k => .lam (fun x => dedup_ (k (n,x)) (n+1) set)
  | .share e k => .share (to_var e) (fun x => dedup_ (k (n,x)) (n+1) set)
  | .is_zero e k => .is_zero (to_var e) (fun x => dedup_ (k (n,x)) (n+1) set)
  | .assert_range w e c => .assert_range w (to_var e) (dedup_ c n ((to_nat e)::set))

def dedup {var} (c : Circuit p (Nat ├Ś var)) : Circuit p var := dedup_ c 0 []

def dedup' (c : Circuit' p) : Circuit' p := fun var => dedup (c (Nat ├Ś var))
```

```tactics
import Clap.Circuit
import Clap.Simulation
import Clap.Id

namespace Clap

/-
  This file introduces an optimization to remove duplicate checks.
  The current version eliminates any `eq0` with an expression that has
  already been met before. The same optimization should be applicable
  to boolean checks, limb checks and other lookup arguments with minor
  modifications.

  Dedup is extremely valuable as it allows to write composable
  circuits with many redundant checks without having to pay the price
  in term of constraints.
-/

namespace Dedup

variable {p : ÔäĽ} [Fact (Nat.Prime p)]

def to_nat {F var : Type} (e : Exp F (ÔäĽ ├Ś var)) : Exp F ÔäĽ :=
  match e with
  | .v (v, _) => .v v
  | .c f => .c f
  | .add l r => to_nat l + to_nat r
  | .mul l r => to_nat l * to_nat r
  | .sub l r => to_nat l - to_nat r

def to_var {F var : Type} (e : Exp F (ÔäĽ ├Ś var)) : Exp F var :=
  match e with
  | .v (_, v) => .v v
  | .c f => .c f
  | .add l r => to_var l + to_var r
  | .mul l r => to_var l * to_var r
  | .sub l r => to_var l - to_var r

def beq : (e1 e2 : Exp (ZMod p) Nat) -> Bool
  | .v n1, .v n2
  | .c n1, .c n2 => n1 = n2
  | .add ll lr, .add rl rr => beq ll rl Ôłž beq lr rr
  | .mul ll lr, .mul rl rr => beq ll rl Ôłž beq lr rr
  | .sub ll lr, .sub rl rr => beq ll rl Ôłž beq lr rr
  | _,_ => false

-- TODO we are mixing expressions from eq0 and assert_range, they should be in different sets
def dedup_ {var} (c : Circuit p (ÔäĽ ├Ś var)) (n : ÔäĽ) (set : List (Exp (ZMod p) ÔäĽ)) : Circuit p var :=
  match c with
  | .nil => .nil
  | .eq0 e c =>
    if (to_nat e) Ôłł set
    then dedup_ c n set
    else .eq0 (to_var e) (dedup_ c n ((to_nat e)::set))
  | .lam k => .lam (fun x => dedup_ (k (n,x)) (n+1) set)
  | .share e k => .share (to_var e) (fun x => dedup_ (k (n,x)) (n+1) set)
  | .is_zero e k => .is_zero (to_var e) (fun x => dedup_ (k (n,x)) (n+1) set)
  | .assert_range w e c => .assert_range w (to_var e) (dedup_ c n ((to_nat e)::set))

def dedup {var} (c : Circuit p (Nat ├Ś var)) : Circuit p var := dedup_ c 0 []

def dedup' (c : Circuit' p) : Circuit' p := fun var => dedup (c (Nat ├Ś var))

namespace Test

abbrev F7 := Clap.F7.F

def a        : Circuit' 7 := fun _ => .lam (fun x => .eq0 (.v x + .c 1) (.eq0 (.v x + .c 2) (.eq0 (.v x + .c 1) .nil )))
def expected : Circuit' 7 := fun _ => .lam (fun x => .eq0 (.v x + .c 1) (.eq0 (.v x + .c 2) .nil ))

#guard s!"{dedup' a Nat}" = s!"{expected Nat}"

end Test

open Id

theorem dedup_sem_pre : ÔłÇ (cl : Circuit p (ZMod p)) (cr : Circuit p (ÔäĽ ├Ś ZMod p)) G,
  wf G cl cr ->
    List.Forall (fun entry => entry.l = (Exp.c entry.r.2).eval) G ->
      cl Ôëł (dedup cr) := by
```

import Clap.Circuit
```tactics
import Clap.Simulation
import Clap.Id

namespace Clap

/-
  This file introduces an optimization to remove duplicate checks.
  The current version eliminates any `eq0` with an expression that has
  already been met before. The same optimization should be applicable
  to boolean checks, limb checks and other lookup arguments with minor
  modifications.

  Dedup is extremely valuable as it allows to write composable
  circuits with many redundant checks without having to pay the price
  in term of constraints.
-/

namespace Dedup

variable {p : ÔäĽ} [Fact (Nat.Prime p)]

def to_nat {F var : Type} (e : Exp F (ÔäĽ ├Ś var)) : Exp F ÔäĽ :=
  match e with
  | .v (v, _) => .v v
  | .c f => .c f
  | .add l r => to_nat l + to_nat r
  | .mul l r => to_nat l * to_nat r
  | .sub l r => to_nat l - to_nat r

def to_var {F var : Type} (e : Exp F (ÔäĽ ├Ś var)) : Exp F var :=
  match e with
  | .v (_, v) => .v v
  | .c f => .c f
  | .add l r => to_var l + to_var r
  | .mul l r => to_mul l * to_mul r
  | .sub l r => to_mul l - to_mul r

def beq : (e1 e2 : Exp (ZMod p) Nat) -> Bool
  | .v n1, .v n2
  | .c n1, .c n2 => n1 = n2
  | .add ll lr, .add rl rr => beq ll rl Ôłž beq lr rr
  | .mul ll lr, .mul rl rr => beq ll rl Ôłž beq lr rr
  | .sub ll lr, .sub rl rr => beq ll rl Ôłž beq lr rr
  | _,_ => false

-- TODO we are mixing expressions from eq0 and assert_range, they should be in different sets
def dedup_ {var} (c : Circuit p (ÔäĽ ├Ś var)) (n : ÔäĽ) (set : List (Exp (ZMod p) ÔäĽ)) : Circuit p var :=
  match c with
  | .nil => .nil
  | .eq0 e c =>
    if (to_nat e) Ôłł set
    then dedup_ c n set
    else .eq0 (to_var e) (dedup_ c n ((to_nat e)::set))
  | .lam k => .lam (fun x => dedup_ (k (n,x)) (n+1) set)
  | .share e k => .share (to_var e) (fun x => dedup_ (k (n,x)) (n+1) set)
  | .is_zero e k => .is_zero (to_var e) (fun x => dedup_ (k (n,x)) (n+1) set)
  | .assert_range w e c => .assert_range w (to_var e) (dedup_ c n ((to_nat e)::set))

def dedup {var} (c : Circuit p (Nat ├Ś var)) : Circuit p var := dedup_ c 0 []

def dedup' (c : Circuit' p) : Circuit' p := fun var => dedup (c (Nat ├Ś var))

namespace Test

abbrev F7 := Clap.F7.F

def a        : Circuit' 7 := fun _ => .lam (fun x => .eq0 (.v x + .c 1) (.eq0 (.v x + .c 2) (.eq0 (.v x + .c 1) .nil )))
def expected : Circuit' 7 := fun _ => .lam (fun x => .eq0 (.v x + .c 1) (.eq0 (.v x + .c 2) .nil ))

#guard s!"{dedup' a Nat}" = s!"{expected Nat}"

end Test

      cl Ôëł (dedup cr) := by
```
```tactics
  intro cl cr G hf h
```

```tactics
  | .assert_range w e c => .assert_range w (to_var e) (dedup_ c n ((to_nat e)::set))
```

```tactics
import Clap.Circuit
import Clap.Simulation
import Clap.Id

namespace Clap

/-
  This file introduces an optimization to remove duplicate checks.
  The current version eliminates any `eq0` with an expression that has
  already been met before. The same optimization should be applicable
  to boolean checks, limb checks and other lookup arguments with minor
  modifications.

  Dedup is extremely valuable as it allows to write composable
  circuits with many redundant checks without having to pay the price
  in term of constraints.
-/

namespace Dedup

variable {p : ÔäĽ} [Fact (Nat.Prime p)]

def to_nat {F var : Type} (e : Exp F (ÔäĽ ├Ś var)) : Exp F ÔäĽ :=
  match e with
  | .v (v, _) => .v v
  | .c f => .c f
  | .add l r => to_nat l + to_nat r
  | .mul l r => to_nat l * to_nat r
  | .sub l r => to_nat l - to_nat r

def to_var {F var : Type} (e : Exp F (ÔäĽ ├Ś var)) : Exp F var :=
  match e with
  | .v (_, v) => .v v
  | .c f => .c f
  | .add l r => to_var l + to_var r
  | .mul l r => to_mul l * to_var r
  | .sub l r => to_var l - to_var r

def beq : (e1 e2 : Exp (ZMod p) Nat) -> Bool
  | .v n1, .v n2
  | .c n1, .c n2 => n1 = n2
  | .add ll lr, .add rl rr => beq ll rl Ôłž beq lr rr
  | .mul ll lr, .mul rl rr => beq ll rl Ôłž beq lr rr
  | .sub ll lr, .sub rl rr => beq ll rl Ôłž beq lr rr
  | _,_ => false

-- TODO we are mixing expressions from eq0 and assert_range, they should be in different sets
def dedup_ {var} (c : Circuit p (ÔäĽ ├Ś var)) (n : ÔäĽ) (set : List (Exp (ZMod p) ÔäĽ)) : Circuit p var :=
  match c with
  | .nil => .nil
  | .eq0 e c =>
    if (to_nat e) Ôłł set
    then dedup_ c n set
    else .eq0 (to_var e) (dedup_ c n ((to_nat e)::set))
  | .lam k => .lam (fun x => dedup_ (k (n,x)) (n+1) set)
  | .share e k => .share (to_var e) (fun x => dedup_ (k (n,x)) (n+1) set)
  | .is_zero e k => .is_zero (to_var e) (fun x => dedup_ (k (n,x)) (n+1) set)
  | .assert_range w e c => .assert_range w (to_var e) (dedup_ c n ((to_nat e)::set))

def dedup {var} (c : Circuit p (Nat ├Ś var)) : Circuit p var := dedup_ c 0 []

def dedup' (c : Circuit' p) : Circuit' p := fun var => dedup (c (Nat ├Ś var))

def a        : Circuit' 7 := fun _ => .lam (fun x => .eq0 (.v x + .c 1) (.eq0 (.v x + .c 2) (.eq0 (.v x + .c 1) .nil )))

#guard s!"{dedup' a Nat}" = s!"{expected Nat}"

end Test
```

```tactics
import Clap.Circuit
import Clap.Simulation
import Clap.Id

namespace Clap

/-
  This file introduces an optimization to remove duplicate checks.
  The current version eliminates any `eq0` with an expression that has
  already been met before. The same optimization should be applicable
  to boolean checks, limb checks and other lookup arguments with minor
  modifications.

  Dedup is extremely valuable as it allows to write composable
  circuits with many redundant checks without having to pay the price
  in term of constraints.
-/

namespace Dedup

variable {p : ÔäĽ} [Fact (Nat.Prime p)]

def to_nat {F var : Type} (e : Exp F (ÔäĽ ├Ś var)) : Exp F ÔäĽ :=
  match e with
  | .v (v, _) => .v v
  | .c f => .c f
  | .add l r => to_nat l + to_nat r
  | .mul l r => to_nat l * to_nat r
  | .sub l r => to_nat l - to_nat r

def to_var {F var : Type} (e : Exp F (ÔäĽ ├Ś var)) : Exp F var :=
  match e with
  | .v (_, v) => .v v
  | .c f => .c f
  | .add l r => to_var l + to_var r
  | .mul l r => to_mul l * to_mul r
  | .sub l r => to_mul l - to_mul r

def beq : (e1 e2 : Exp (ZMod p) Nat) -> Bool
  | .v n1, .v n2
  | .c n1, .c n2 => n1 = n2
  | .add ll lr, .add rl rr => beq ll rl Ôłž beq lr rr
  | .mul ll lr, .mul rl rr => beq ll rl Ôłž beq lr rr
  | .sub ll lr, .sub rl rr => beq ll rl Ôłž beq lr rr
  | _,_ => false

-- TODO we are mixing expressions from eq0 and assert_range, they should be in different sets
def dedup_ {var} (c : Circuit p (ÔäĽ ├Ś var)) (n : ÔäĽ) (set : List (Exp (ZMod p) ÔäĽ)) : Circuit p var :=
  match c with
  | .nil => .nil
  | .eq0 e c =>
    if (to_nat e) Ôłł set
    then dedup_ c n set
    else .eq0 (to_var e) (dedup_ c n ((to_nat e)::set))
  | .lam k => .lam (fun x => dedup_ (k (n,x)) (n+1) set)
  | .share e k => .share (to_var e) (fun x => dedup_ (k (n,x)) (n+1) set)
  | .is_zero e k => .is_zero (to_var e) (fun x => dedup_ (k (n,x)) (n+1) set)
  | .assert_range w e c => .assert_range w (to_var e) (dedup_ c n ((to_nat e)::set))

def dedup {var} (c : Circuit p (Nat ├Ś var)) : Circuit p var := dedup_ c 0 []

def dedup' (c : Circuit' p) : Circuit' p := fun var => dedup (c (Nat ├Ś var))

namespace Test

abbrev F7 := Clap.F7.F

def a        : Circuit' 7 := fun _ => .lam (fun x => .eq0 (.v x + .c 1) (.eq0 (.v x + .c 2) (.eq0 (.v x + .c 1) .nil )))
def expected : Circuit' 7 := fun _ => .lam (fun x => .eq0 (.v x + .c 1) (.eq0 (.v x + .c 2) .nil ))

#guard s!"{dedup' a Nat}" = s!"{expected Nat}"

end Test
```
